{"key_event":"Napoleonic fiscal-military expansion and Act of Union","reasoning":"The wartime state markedly increased taxation, borrowing, military mobilization, surveillance, census-taking, naval reach, and direct control over Ireland, pushing an already strong state higher. Civil society was mixed: abolitionist, evangelical, petitioning, commercial, and volunteer networks grew, but repression of radicals, Combination Acts, and loss of Irish institutional autonomy meant only a slight net weakening from the prior period.","society_change":-0.2,"society_power":6.1,"state_change":0.7,"state_power":7.7}
